speaker: <title>
tf: 事務員を志望しています

speaker: 倒れた男
tf:
You slipped through every trap and are about to kill me... I'll admit，
as an assassin，you're the better one...

speaker: 倒れた男
tf: But it was all for nothing. You no longer have a place to call home!

speaker: アヤメ
tf: Oh，is that so? Thank you for the advice.

speaker: 倒れた男
tf: Your composure won't last long. You're all finished!
A loud explosion rang out.

speaker: アヤメ
tf:
I'm sorry for cutting you off. If we ever meet again，please tell me
the rest.

speaker: アヤメ
tf: Well，the job's done. Shall we return to the office?
A massive explosion rocked the building.

speaker: アヤメ
tf: Fufu，it's quite lively... Oh?

speaker: アヤメ
tf: It's the office that's on fire，isn't it. Well... what shall we do?
<size=48>A few days later...</size>

speaker: <user>
tf:
Thought so... It's a small amount，but the dispatch records and
inventory don't match.

speaker: 倉庫警備の兵士
tf:
So，the warehouse goods have gone missing，is that what you're
saying?

speaker: <user>
tf:
If you want to put it euphemistically，yes. But I think another word
fits better.

speaker: 倉庫警備の兵士
tf: Another word，you mean?

speaker: <user>
tf: Embezzlement.

speaker: 倉庫警備の兵士
tf:
Th-that's an exaggeration! Even the Commander shouldn't say such
things!

speaker: <user>
tf: Are you in a position to say that?

speaker: 倉庫警備の兵士
tf: ...I apologize.

speaker: 倉庫警備の兵士
tf:
...*tsk*
You're just a smooth-talking punk protected by a woman.

speaker: <user>
tf:
In this Frontline Base，everything is precious—even the smallest
supplies.

speaker: 倉庫警備の兵士
tf: Y-yes，sir!

speaker: <user>
tf: Then you know what you need to do，right?

speaker: 倉庫警備の兵士
tf: Yes，sir! I'll correct it immediately!

speaker: <user>
tf: You should handle this much without being told. *sigh* What a drag.

speaker: <user>
tf:
Do guys like that become my responsibility if they die? I'd think dying
to a Calamity is an accident，but...

speaker: <user>
tf:
Well，in this understaffed Frontline Base，there aren't many guys we
can afford to lose.

speaker: アリシア
tf: Commanderrr... *whimper* there you are. I have a favor to ask...

speaker: <user>
tf: What? If it's a pain，I'm turning you down.

speaker: アリシア
tf:
Well，um... it might be a hassle，but I'd like you to interview a
volunteer for the base.

speaker: <user>
tf: Do I really have to do that? If she's promising，just hire her yourself.

speaker: アリシア
tf:
That's the thing... she's someone I find hard to judge. Could I leave
the decision to you?

speaker: <user>
tf:
...Someone even you can't figure out? Sounds interesting. I'll meet
her.

speaker: アリシア
tf: Yes，please. Um... be careful...

speaker: <user>
tf: ...Be careful? What kind of person is she?

speaker: <user>
tf: You're the applicant? I'm the Commander of this base.

speaker: アヤメ
tf:
My name is Ayame. It is an honor to be interviewed by the Lord
Commander.

speaker: <user>
tf:
We're short-staffed. I'll be looking over your application as we
talk—hope you don't mind.

speaker: アヤメ
tf: Of course. I look forward to it.

speaker: <user>
tf:
This isn't a typical interview. Unless you have a compelling reason
otherwise，you'll end up working here.

speaker: アヤメ
tf:
Oh，I am delighted. I came prepared for a one-way trip，so this is very
helpful.

speaker: <user>
tf:
Now，the paperwork... says you're applying for a clerk position.
You're an experienced hire，right?

speaker: アヤメ
tf:
Yes，I worked as a clerk at my previous job as well. I hope I can put
my skills to use here.

speaker: <user>
tf: What kind of work did you mainly do at your previous job?

speaker: アヤメ
tf:
It was primarily cleaning，such as taking out the trash and sorting
documents.

speaker: <user>
tf: Yeah，that's what we're looking for. You can jump right in.

speaker: <user>
tf:
(Nothing strange about her at all. Alicia，why did you leave this one
to me?)

speaker: <user>
tf:
Why did you leave your previous job? And why come all the way to the
Frontline Base?

speaker: アヤメ
tf:
Well，actually... my previous workplace suffered an explosion and
burned down.

speaker: <user>
tf: Huh，explosion... fire?

speaker: アヤメ
tf:
Yes，the members are missing as well. And I have not even received
my wages.

speaker: <user>
tf: An explosion isn't that unusual... but，'members'?

speaker: アヤメ
tf: I am embarrassed to admit it，but my reason for working is money.

speaker: アヤメ
tf:
I thought here I could get paid properly，and it did not seem likely to
explode easily.

speaker: <user>
tf: I can't say it'll never explode or catch fire. But that aside，it's fine.

speaker: <user>
tf:
Next，I need to confirm your qualifications and skills. Your
application lists assassination and calligraphy...

speaker: <user>
tf: ...Wait，what does this mean?

speaker: アヤメ
tf:
Yes，I am from the Thousand Nations Alliance，Hourai. I have been
practicing brush calligraphy since childhood.

speaker: アヤメ
tf: I listed it on my application as a skill that could be useful as a clerk.

speaker: <user>
tf: No，that's not what I want to ask about.

speaker: <user>
tf: Assassination? What do you mean? Where's the clerk?

speaker: アヤメ
tf:
Yes，I was a clerk. That means I can assassinate anyone without any
problem.

speaker: <user>
tf:
I have no idea what you're talking about，but let me correct you:
assassination isn't part of a clerk's job.

speaker: アヤメ
tf: Oh... so the clerks here do not handle assassinations?

speaker: <user>
tf: No. Of course not. No base has that.

speaker: アヤメ
tf: Um，then what kind of work is the 'cleaning' that clerks do?

speaker: <user>
tf: It just means cleaning the workplace，plain and simple.

speaker: アヤメ
tf:
So it is not about removing people who are troublesome or irritating
by killing them to clean things up...?

speaker: <user>
tf: No clerk's job is ever that violent!

speaker: アヤメ
tf: Oh? So then，what about paperwork...?

speaker: <user>
tf:
It means arranging documents by type or date so they're easy to
access.

speaker: アヤメ
tf:
So it is not about going to the homes I 'cleaned'，finding the required
documents，and delivering them to the client...?

speaker: <user>
tf:
So you were stealing information from the people you killed... That's
assassin work.

speaker: アヤメ
tf: Wait，could it be that I was working as an assassin?

speaker: <user>
tf:
That seems to be the case. More to the point，how did a clerk end up
killing people?

speaker: アヤメ
tf:
Well，I was told it was a sanitation service for society，cleaning up
unnecessary trash...

speaker: <user>
tf:
That's one violently aggressive cleaning service... Were you working
for anti-government terrorists?

speaker: アヤメ
tf:
I only looked at the salary and applied without checking the job
classification，so I do not know what the work involved.

speaker: アヤメ
tf:
But everyone there was very kind. Though there were whispers that it
was a company that dealt in assassination.

speaker: <user>
tf:
That's not a rumor，it's just the truth! Don't take an assassin at their
word!

speaker: アヤメ
tf: S-So that's how it was...?!

speaker: <user>
tf:
The ones who made you do that are messed up too. What kind of
assassination group was that?

speaker: <user>
tf:
(Or did they send Ayame in as a disposable pawn? And she happened
to show talent?)

speaker: アヤメ
tf: Th-Then，does that mean I failed the trial?

speaker: <user>
tf: From what I've heard，you're not suitable as a clerk，but...

speaker: <user>
tf: (But it is a waste to let her go.)

speaker: <user>
tf:
(An assassin who delivered results and survived. Since Alicia didn't
stop her，she's probably not dangerous... I suppose.)

speaker: アヤメ
tf: Well，if it is acceptable，could you perhaps give me a trial period?

speaker: <user>
tf: Hm? What do you mean by 'trial period'?

speaker: アヤメ
tf: Lord Commander，is there anyone you would like me to clean up?

speaker: <user>
tf: When you say 'clean'，you mean...

speaker: アヤメ
tf: ...Yes. If you give the order，I will kill anyone.

speaker: アヤメ
tf: How about it? Is there no one you wish dead?
